# Yet Another Dependency Injection Container

Take a look to the tests for usage examples

Quickstart (in russian): <https://bitbucket.org/astynax/yadic/wiki/intro>

modules.name: yadic.dot
documentation: coding:utf-8

functions.name: yadic.dot.dot
documentation:
Builds the .dot-file for container's hierarchy

```
param container

container object

type container

yadic.container.Container

param include

include list [(group, entity),...]

type include

dict

param exclude

exclude list [(group, entity),...]

type exclude

dict
```

functions.name: yadic.dot._key_pairs
documentation:
Returns list of paris which contains
toplevel and second level keys of source dict.

```pycon
>>> list(sorted(_key_pairs({'a', {'x': 1, 'y': 2}, 'b', {}})))
[('a', 'x'), ('a', 'y')]
```

functions.name: yadic.dot._render_digraph
documentation: Renders paris of nodes to .dot-file with directional graph.

functions.name: yadic.dot._parse_filter
documentation:
Parses the filter-string (include/exclude).

Returns the dictionary in form "group -> set of entities"

```pycon
>>> _parse_string('names:Tom,Moe;pets:Spot;cars')
{'names': set(['Tom', 'Moe']), 'pets': set(['Spot']), 'cars': set()}
```

modules.name: yadic
documentation: -*- coding:utf-8 -*-

modules.name: yadic.util
documentation: Utilties
coding:utf-8

functions.name: yadic.util.merge
documentation:
Updates the dict "d1" with elems of the dict "d2",
and returns the dict "d1".
Keys like 'a' and '$a' will be considered equal!
Collisions will be resolved using the function "fn",
which takes:
- both of values
- link to itself
- path to values (for ex. ("level1", "level2"))
end returns new value.

functions.name: yadic.util.deep_merge
documentation: Merges d2 into d1 on any level of the depth
